layman_headline: The market is fearful and most altcoins look beaten up — but none have a clean enough setup to buy right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a 'Fear' state with Bitcoin dominance high at 56.6%, meaning money is flowing to Bitcoin and away from smaller coins. Several altcoins have dropped sharply, but their charts are still showing strong downward momentum with bearish patterns. Ethereum is the most interesting candidate — it's down 61% from its all-time high with a low RSI, and has bounced 8% this week — but it hasn't confirmed a reversal yet and is still below all its moving averages. Jumping in now would be catching a falling knife.
layman_action: Wait. Don't buy anything yet. Set an alert for Ethereum around $1,810 (near support) and watch for a confirmed bounce with increasing volume before entering. Patience in fearful markets pays off.
expert_reasoning: Every swing candidate has a bearish chart bias score. BONK is at -2.0 (strongly bearish with Three Black Crows), NIGHT dropped 37% in a week without any support confirmation, and MON/KITE are in persistent downtrends below all MAs. LDO has the only bullish chart (+0.85) with Three White Soldiers, but its RSI at 59 and +51.7% 5-day momentum signal it's already extended — the risk/reward is just 0.38x to resistance at $0.40. ETH at RSI 39 with $1,800 support nearby is the cleanest potential setup, but it's still below all moving averages and the chart bias is -0.70. The RSI signal source has an 80% win rate historically, but I need it to combine with a bullish chart confirmation (MACD cross or break above SMA20) before deploying capital. BTC dominance at 56.6% is another headwind for alt positions.
risk_factors: BTC dominance continues rising, draining liquidity from all altcoins further
ETH support at $1,800 fails, triggering another leg down toward $1,600
Macro uncertainty (no macro data available) could surprise to the downside
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — does it hold and form a higher low?
BTC breaking above $67,000 toward $70K — would signal broader risk-on shift
LDO for a pullback entry near $0.35 if its bullish momentum pauses — best chart structure of all candidates
market_context: The market is in Fear (29/100) but improving from last week's 25.9 average. BTC is up 5% on the week and holding $65K, but high dominance at 56.6% means altcoins are underperforming — not ideal for alt swing trades.
context_banner: Fear is improving but altcoin charts remain bearish across the board — discipline demands waiting for a confirmed reversal setup before deploying capital.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal (80% win rate) — but needs chart confirmation before acting on it.
Fear index at 29 improving from 25.9 weekly average suggests sentiment may be bottoming, but hasn't flipped to greed yet.
Bitcoin eyeing $70K rally and defying tech sell-off is a positive macro catalyst that could eventually lift alts — our second most reliable signal source (67% win rate).
ETH derivatives reset signals potential fresh positioning opportunity once spot price confirms a reversal.
All candidates except LDO have bearish chart bias scores (-0.35 to -2.0). LDO is bullish (+0.85) but already extended with poor risk/reward (0.38x).
